item.title: Are A.I.-Generated Videos Changing How We See Animals?
item.description: By manipulating animals to do wonderful things, we may become numb to their real wonder.

item.title: Rebecca Solnit Says the Left’s Next Hero Is Already Here
item.description: The writer and activist on how political change happens and taking the long view.

item.title: My Sister’s Crime Shattered Our Family. Do I Have to Help Her?
item.description: If I refuse, her future looks shakier. If I help, my family may never forgive me.

item.title: The Best Things on Fifth Avenue Don’t Cost a Dime
item.description: Trying on clothes you can’t afford is an act of transgression — and liberation.

item.title: The Race to Stop Wildlife Trafficking in Africa
item.description: In Nigeria, customs officers and conservationists are confronting the grim impacts of the $20 billion trade.

item.title: X’s Chatbot Started Undressing Women. Was This What A.I. Wanted All Along?
item.description: Grok Imagine’s “nudify” scandal reveals something about the dream of manhandling photos.

item.title: Should I Tell My Estranged Brother About His Biological Father?
item.description: I’m less troubled by my mother’s affair than by her decision to join a genealogy website where this could so easily surface.

item.title: Can I Stare at My Girlfriend?
item.description: A ruling on the line between creepy and sweet.

item.title: How Tragedy, Wealth and Trump Shaped JB Pritzker
item.description: The governor of Illinois and Trump antagonist has become a national figure for Democrats. Where will that lead?

item.title: My Sex Addiction Nearly Wrecked Our Marriage. Do We Have to Tell the Kids?
item.description: My wife worries that withholding such a significant fact becomes an ongoing deception that could undermine trust.

item.title: Coding After Coders: The End of Computer Programming as We Know It
item.description: In the era of A.I. agents, many Silicon Valley programmers are now barely programming. Instead, what they’re doing is deeply, deeply weird.

item.title: My Mother’s Dying Wish Took Me on a Trip I’ll Never Forget
item.description: What I thought was a burden was a tether across death’s divide.

item.title: How Escape Rooms Helped Me Escape My Life
item.description: They allowed me to glimpse a future version of myself in a reality different than my own — one that might actually be OK.

item.title: I’ve Spent Years Exploring the Grand Canyon. A Fire Revealed Something New.
item.description: A devastating wildfire forever changed the rare beauty of the secluded North Rim.
